name: Daydream
history: A flatground trick where the board does a half-flip to the heel side and is then kicked back to normal. An obscure Rodney Mullen-influenced trick.

name: Switch Bigspin
history: A bigspin performed in switch stance. The full 360 body rotation combined with the board rotation from the non-dominant stance makes this very technical.

name: Nollie Bigspin
history: A bigspin from nollie stance. The nose is popped and both body and board complete 360-degree backside rotations.

name: Nollie Frontside Bigspin
history: A frontside bigspin from nollie stance. The body and board rotate frontside 360 from a nollie pop.

name: Fakie Frontside Bigspin
history: A frontside bigspin from fakie stance. Combines the reverse riding direction with the frontside 360 spin combination.

name: Nollie 360 Frontside Flip
history: A frontside 360 kickflip from nollie stance. One of the most obscure and technical nollie spin-flip combinations.

name: Switch Big Flip
history: A big flip (backside 360 body + kickflip) in switch stance. An extremely rare switch combination trick.

name: Ghetto Bird Backside
history: A backside version of the ghetto bird. The body rotates backside 180 while the board kickflips.

name: Nollie Tre Flip
history: A 360 flip from nollie stance. The nollie pop drives the backside 360 shove-it with kickflip combination.

name: Fakie Tre Flip
history: A 360 flip from fakie stance. Some skaters find this more intuitive than the regular tre flip.

name: Switch Tre Flip
history: A 360 flip in switch stance. One of the most coveted switch tricks and a benchmark for technical skating ability.

name: Noseblunt Stall
history: A stall on the coping where the nose of the board wedges against the top edge of the coping while the front wheels hang over. A technical lip trick on vert and mini ramps.
youtube_query: nose blunt stall skateboard ramp tutorial

name: Blunt Fakie (Ramp)
history: A blunt stall on coping that exits back into the ramp fakie. A fundamental ramp trick in mini ramp and vert skating.

name: Smith to Fakie
history: A Smith grind on coping that exits fakie back into the ramp. A more stylish variation of the standard Smith exit.
youtube_query: smith grind to fakie ramp skateboard tutorial

name: Nosegrind to Shove-it Out
history: A nosegrind that exits with a pop shove-it. The 180-degree board rotation is added at the end of the grind.
youtube_query: nosegrind shove it out skateboard tutorial

name: 50-50 to Kickflip Out
history: A 50-50 grind that exits with a kickflip. Adding the flip trick to the grind exit greatly increases difficulty.

name: Smith to Kickflip Out
history: A Smith grind that exits with a kickflip. A very technical ledge combo.
youtube_query: smith grind kickflip out skateboard tutorial

name: Crooked to Heelflip Out
history: A crooked grind that exits with a heelflip. Combining the technical K-grind with a heel flip exit.

name: Feeble to Kickflip Out
history: A feeble grind that exits with a kickflip. An advanced rail trick that blends grind and flip technique.
youtube_query: feeble grind kickflip out skateboard tutorial

name: Ollie to Nosegrind
history: A clean ollie entry into a nosegrind. The foundational approach to nosegrinding a ledge or rail.

name: Switch Lipslide
history: A lipslide in switch stance. The non-dominant approach adds significant difficulty to the already blind-commitment trick.

name: Nollie Lipslide
history: A lipslide entered from nollie stance. The nose is popped and the board goes over the obstacle into the slide.

name: Fakie Nosegrind
history: A nosegrind entered from fakie stance. Rolling backward into a nosegrind is particularly challenging for spatial awareness.

name: Fakie 5-0 Grind
history: A 5-0 grind entered from fakie stance. The backward approach changes the timing and mechanics of entering the grind.

name: Frontside 180 Nosegrind
history: A frontside 180 spin into a nosegrind. The rotation sets up the grind position naturally in a smooth flow.

name: Backside 180 Nosegrind
history: A backside 180 that lands in a nosegrind. One of the classic smooth transitions from spin to grind.

name: Frontside 180 50-50
history: A frontside 180 into a 50-50 grind. The rotation is used to approach the obstacle from a different angle.

name: Backside 180 50-50
history: A backside 180 into a 50-50 grind. A classic combo trick combining the 180 spin with the fundamental grind.

name: Switch Overcrook
history: An overcrook grind in switch stance. One of the most technical switch grinds due to the nose-over-the-obstacle position.

name: Fakie Crooked Grind
history: A crooked grind entered from fakie stance. The reverse approach makes locking the front truck into the K-grind position very challenging.

name: Frontside Crooks
history: A frontside crooked grind. The nose angles toward the skater as they grind frontside along the obstacle.

name: Nollie Bluntslide
history: A bluntslide entered from nollie stance. The nose is popped and the tail drops into the slide position.

name: Fakie Bluntslide
history: A bluntslide entered from fakie stance. Rolling backward before dropping the tail into the slide.

name: Switch Blunt Grind
history: A blunt grind performed in switch stance. Among the rarest switch lip tricks.

name: Cab to Grind
history: A fakie 360 (Caballerial) that lands in a grind. Combining the classic Cab spin with a ledge grind.
youtube_query: cab to grind skateboard trick tutorial

name: Nollie Flip to Tail
history: A nollie kickflip that lands in a tailstall on an obstacle. A creative flip-to-lip trick combination.
youtube_query: nollie flip to tail stall skateboard

name: Halfcab Flip
history: A fakie backside 180 combined with a kickflip (landing in regular). A natural combination of the half cab with a flip trick.

name: Halfcab Heelflip
history: A half cab combined with a heelflip. The backside 180 and heel flip complement each other naturally.

name: Halfcab Kickflip
history: A fakie 180 combined with a kickflip. A reliable and visually appealing trick in modern street skating.

name: 360 Pop Shove-it
history: The board completes a full 360-degree backside rotation via a shove-it motion. A foundational spinning board trick.

name: Frontside Kickflip (Curved Rail)
history: A kickflip performed to lock onto a curved or curved stair rail frontside. Adapts the kickflip entry to more complex architectural features.

name: Drop-In
history: The act of entering a vert ramp, half-pipe, or bowl from the top by pressing the tail on the coping and leaning in. A fundamental vert skill.
youtube_query: how to drop in skateboard ramp tutorial

name: Pump
history: Using leg extension and compression in a curved transition to generate speed without pushing. The fundamental energy source for transition skating.
youtube_query: how to pump skateboard transition tutorial

name: Snap
history: A powerful, precise ollie pop where the tail slaps the ground cleanly. Considered the foundation of all modern street skating technique.

name: Nollie Sal Flip
history: A nollie version of the sal flip (frontside 180 heelflip). Named after Sal Barbier who popularized the regular version.

name: Sal Flip
history: A frontside 180 heelflip. Named after Sal Barbier who standardized the name in the early 1990s street skating scene.
youtube_query: sal flip skateboard tutorial frontside 180 heelflip

name: Fakie Sal Flip
history: A sal flip (frontside 180 heelflip) from fakie stance. Adding the fakie position to an already demanding trick.

name: Backside Heelflip
history: A heelflip combined with a backside 180 body rotation. The body rotates backside while the board heelflips.

name: Frontside Heelflip
history: A heelflip combined with a frontside 180 body rotation. Also called a sal flip in some regions.
